_comment: Key illustrated events for Chapter 4 (VERIFIED-ONLY chapter). 'anchor' is an EXACT substring of chapters/ch04.md. 'refs' are entity slugs from reference/index.json. 'image' filled by tools/refgen.py AFTER sign-off. Each event depicts a documented happening (1636 collapse / Bijapur service in the Karnataka / the family's move to Pune) — no invented scenes.

events.title: An honourable defeat (1636)
events.anchor: He made terms, and laid down the fight.
events.scene_prompt: 1636: the end of Shahaji's long losing war. Shahaji — a dignified bearded Maratha warrior-noble in his tall plumed Deccan turban and robe — stands tall but defeated before the overwhelming power he could not beat: ranks of heavily-armoured Mughal/Deccan-sultanate cavalry and war-elephants and banners stretching away under a hard sky, a battered hill-fort behind him. He is making terms — proud and unbroken in bearing even in surrender. Solemn, grand, no fighting or gore; the weight of a brave cause lost.

events.title: Into service, far to the south
events.anchor: far to the south, into the Karnataka country
events.scene_prompt: Shahaji, now in the service of Bijapur, rides away south toward the distant Karnataka with his Maratha retinue — mounted on his grey war-horse, escorted by lean Mavala riders, a long road unspooling into a hazy southern landscape of hills and open country. A capable soldier going to do a hard duty far from home. Wide, with a sense of great distance and departure.

events.title: The road to Pune
events.scene_prompt: 1636: Jijabai and six-year-old Shivaji leave the great hill-fort of Shivneri (visible behind them) and travel west toward Pune — Jijabai dignified, the small boy beside her, a curtained palki and a few servants and Maratha escort on a winding hill road through the Maval country. Leaving the safe rock for an unknown, ruined town. Hopeful and resolute, a new beginning.
